name: Valsurbio from Camporredondo de Alba — circular by the Arroyo de la Cárcava
name_original: Valsurbio desde Camporredondo de Alba — circular por el Arroyo de la Cárcava
region_intro: Hiking around Velilla Del Río Carrión offers access to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, a region characterized by high mountain peaks, river valleys, and diverse forests. The area features prominent summits like Pico Espigüete and Pico Murcia, alongside the Carrión River and its tributaries. Indigenous pine forests, oak groves, and unique juniper stands contribute to the varied terrain. This natural environment provides a range of hiking experiences, from riverside paths to challenging alpine ascents. Best hiking trails in Velilla Del Río Carrión The most popular hiking route is Mazobre Waterfall Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) moderate trail that takes about 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This path leads to the Cascada de Mazobre, Palencia's largest waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Prieta, Peña del infierno y Mojón de Tres Provincias — circular por el Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a difficult 10.3 miles (16.5 km) path. This circular route traverses high mountain terrain within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. Local hikers also love the Espigüete by the East ridge — circular in the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail leading through high mountain landscapes, often completed in about 6 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Velilla Del Río Carrión is defined by high mountain peaks, river valleys, and diverse forests within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. The region offers routes suitable for easy walks, moderate excursions, and difficult alpine challenges. The routes in Velilla Del Río Carrión are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Velilla Del Río Carrión's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Velilla Del Río Carrión?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around Velilla Del Río Carrión. These include a variety of options, with 3 easy trails, 9 moderate trails, and 9 difficult trails, catering to different experience levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Velilla Del Río Carrión offers several easy trails perfect for families or beginners. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093061098/valsurbio-from-camporredondo-de-alba-circular-by-the-arroyo-de-la-carcava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valsurbio from Camporredondo de Alba — circular by the Arroyo de la Cárcava&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) circular route with gentle elevation changes, offering pleasant scenery.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in this region?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, being a gateway to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. You can expect to see high mountain peaks like Pico Espigüete and Pico Murcia, the Carrión River, and diverse forests including indigenous pine forests (some with unique trees like the 'Pino Enroscado'), oak groves, and juniper stands. The area also features the impressive Cascada de Mazobre, Palencia's largest waterfall.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Velilla Del Río Carrión?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For a challenging circular experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068265978/pena-prieta-pena-del-infierno-y-mojon-de-tres-provincias-circular-por-el-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Prieta, Peña del infierno y Mojón de Tres Provincias — circular por el Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 10.3-mile (16.5 km) trail traversing high mountain terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Hikers can discover various points of interest. The area is known for its mountain passes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3593173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Glorio Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Picones&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find stunning vistas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/814184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Picos de Europa from Collado de Llesba&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corzo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by historical elements, such as the ruins of the old Besande anthracite mine.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Velilla Del Río Carrión?
A: The trails in Velilla Del Río Carrión are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to serene river valleys, and the opportunity to explore the Montaña Palentina Natural Park's rich ecosystem.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to waterfalls?
A: Yes, one of the most popular routes in the area leads directly to a significant waterfall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068211033/mazobre-waterfall-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazobre Waterfall Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.9-mile (6.3 km) path that takes you to the Cascada de Mazobre, which is Palencia's largest waterfall.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the prominent peaks like Pico Espigüete or Pico Murcia?
A: Absolutely. The region is defined by its impressive peaks. For an immersive experience with Pico Espigüete, consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068198404/espigueete-by-the-east-ridge-circular-in-the-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espigüete by the East ridge — circular in the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;. To experience Pico Murcia, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068286621/pico-murcia-from-cardano-de-arriba-circular-through-the-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Murcia from Cardaño de Arriba — circular through the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging route with stunning views.

Q: Are there any huts or shelters available along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, for longer treks or multi-day adventures, there are several huts and shelters in the vicinity. Options include a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5837344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Free shelter in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5144390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue de Villaverde&lt;/a&gt;, and a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3943784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bivouac at the Cardaño Needles&lt;/a&gt;, among others.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Velilla Del Río Carrión?
A: The Montaña Palentina region offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with blooming flora or rich fall colors. Summer is ideal for higher altitude trails, while winter can offer challenging but rewarding snowshoeing or ski mountaineering experiences, especially in the higher peaks, though conditions require appropriate gear and experience.

Q: Are there any educational or interpretive centers in the area?
A: Yes, for those interested in the local wildlife, the Center for the Interpretation of the Trout provides information about the habits and habitat of this species, offering an educational stop for visitors to learn more about the aquatic ecosystems of the region.
tips: Uninhabited town since the 1960s, probably due to the harshness of living at more than 1,450 meters above sea level with little communication by road, no electricity and harsh winters.
